rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level signal routing bindings.
Use component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports and features.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type annexes for component types, implementation annexes for implementations.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible ports and features only, avoiding implementation-specific subcomponent paths in type contracts.
Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes at appropriate scopes without altering component structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Implementation-level bindings require placement in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level signal routing bindings in component type annexes.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structural elements
